page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 24 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and a massive 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is supported by a 38 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results highlight exceptional memory and database performance. The server achieves top-tier status in PassMark database operations and memory score, supported by a large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 378.34 GB/sec. CPU performance is strong, with high marks in Geekbench multi-core tasks, software compilation, and bzip3 decompression. However, the system exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning workloads, showing poor performance in LLM text generation for small models and prompt processing for 70B parameter models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture combined with high-density local NVMe storage delivers high resource value for data-intensive applications without the overhead of x86 licensing. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable per-core performance. This server is highly suited for large-scale transactional databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation pipelines, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 192.0 GB of system memory and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, backed by a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in database operations and memory bandwidth, alongside strong software compilation and multi-core CPU capabilities. However, it shows performance trade-offs in large-scale LLM inference tasks. The ARM64 architecture provides excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy workloads. This instance is highly suited for high-throughput databases, web serving, and data-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 server featuring 24 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 15 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge features 24 ARM64 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 15 TB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring massive local NVMe capacity and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 24 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
15,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read-only bandwidth
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and web serving
